contract_claim: The document's Pattern 2 (Error Propagation) states 'The MCP server (src/server.ts) may also throw McpError directly' and implies typed errors from the backend propagate as MCP protocol errors. However, the shadow doc for server.ts reveals a different pattern: 'Session-related errors (terminated, not found, proxy not running) are caught per-tool and returned as { success: false, error } JSON rather than thrown; other errors are rethrown as McpError.' This means typed session errors are actually caught and converted to structured failure responses at the server layer, not propagated as protocol-level MCP errors.
observed_behavior: Session-related errors (terminated, not found, proxy not running) are caught per-tool and returned as `{ success: false, error }` JSON rather than thrown; other errors are rethrown as `McpError`.
